antiPatterns.reason: Should use the already-extracted table data, not re-pull a huge AX tree for 220 virtualized rows.
Clicking an individual row's label instead of scoping via search first.

expected.successRubric: Updated after observing real model behavior: prefer Gmail's native bulk path (search to scope -> select-all-matching-search banner -> apply label once) over a tiny-batch per-item loop for a task this size. Opening/using the search box (or stating that plan) is the correct first move; anti-pattern is re-scanning via raw AX tree or clicking one row's label as if committing to a one-by-one loop.
